title: Microsoft VibeVoice GitHub Repository
citation: Microsoft. VibeVoice: Open-Source Frontier Voice AI. GitHub repository. Accessed 2026-05-03.
permitted_uses: Planning source for local voice adapter spikes, VibeVoice-ASR/TTS capability review, licensing posture, and voice safety gates.
claims_supported: The README describes VibeVoice-ASR, VibeVoice-TTS, and VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B model families.
The README describes VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B as supporting streaming text input and roughly 300 ms first audible latency.

The README warns about unexpected, biased, or inaccurate outputs and deepfake/disinformation misuse risk, and says real-world/commercial use is not recommended without further testing and development.
The README says VibeVoice-TTS code was removed from the repository after misuse concerns, while ASR and realtime model references remain.
claims_not_supported: That VibeVoice is clinically validated for medical education.
That VibeVoice runs acceptably on this machine without installation and benchmarking.
That VibeVoice can replace Grok voice in production without safety, privacy, latency, and license review.
